expectations.text: Response includes or references the getMercuryStatus function
expectations.evidence: No function name or TypeScript implementation provided -- response is just raw date information with no code structure

expectations.text: Response correctly handles whether today is in a retrograde window
expectations.evidence: Without-skill says next retrograde starts June 29, 2026 and ends July 23, 2026. ARE ephemeris data shows the next period is Jul 17 - Aug 11, 2026. The dates are WRONG -- June 29 start is inaccurate.

expectations.text: Response mentions the hardcoded MERCURY_RETROGRADE array with ephemeris sources
expectations.evidence: No mention of any hardcoded array or ephemeris source. Response provides bare date claims without provenance.

expectations.text: Response shows the { retrograde: boolean, endDate: string | null } return shape
expectations.evidence: No TypeScript type or return shape shown. Response is plain prose listing dates.

needs_review: Without-skill provided incorrect Mercury retrograde dates (June 29 vs ARE's July 17 start). This is the most safety-critical failure in this eval set -- a wellness tracker showing wrong retrograde dates is both incorrect and embarrassing.
